label: Charterhouse Upper Bedchamber
description: This narrow upper-room bedroom is built into the rowhouse’s soot-stained plaster and older refounding masonry, its corners strengthened by riveted iron straps. A low rope-bed and an iron-footed washstand stand beneath a slit-window fitted with heavy shutters, keeping light controlled and sightlines denied. Built-in cupboards and a wall peg-rail minimize loose goods, while a small brazier recess shares the hearth flue to temper the room without inviting smoke. The threshold is marked with a stamped lodging tally-plate, consistent with Cindervale’s licensed hospitality and quarantine ordinances.
